info.title: Unsloth UI Backend
info.description: Backend API for Unsloth UI - Training and Model Management

get.description:
Check whether auth has already been initialized.

- initialized = False -> frontend should wait for the seeded admin bootstrap.
- initialized = True  -> frontend should show login or force the first password change.

post.description: Login with username/password and receive access + refresh tokens.

Exchange a valid refresh token for a new access token.

The refresh token itself is reusable until it expires (7 days).
Allow the authenticated user to replace the default password.

get.description: List all API keys for the authenticated user (raw keys are never exposed).

post.description: Create a new API key. The raw key is returned once and cannot be retrieved later.

schema.title: Response Revoke Api Key Api Auth Api Keys  Key Id  Delete

get.description:
Get a live snapshot of GPU hardware utilization.

Designed to be polled by the frontend during training.
Returns live GPU memory usage information for the active backend.

post.description:
Start a training job.

This endpoint initiates training in the background and returns immediately.
Use the /status endpoint to check training progress.

Stop the currently running training job.

Body:
    save (bool): If True (default), save the model at the current checkpoint.
Reset training state so the user can return to configuration.

get.description: Get the current training status.
Get training metrics (loss, learning rate, steps).

Stream training progress updates using Server-Sent Events (SSE).

This endpoint provides real-time updates on training progress.
Supports reconnection via the SSE spec:
  - Sends `id:` with each event so the browser tracks position.
  - Sends `retry:` to control reconnection interval.
  - Sends named `event:` types (progress, heartbeat, complete, error).
  - Reads `Last-Event-ID` header on reconnect to replay missed steps.

List local model candidates from custom models dir, HF cache,
legacy Unsloth HF cache, and LM Studio directories.

parameters.name: models_dir

schema.description: Directory to scan for local model folders
schema.title: Models Dir

parameters.description: Directory to scan for local model folders

get.description: List all registered custom model scan folders.

post.description: Register a new directory to scan for local models.

delete.description: Remove a registered custom scan folder.

get.description:
Return well-known model directories that exist on this machine.

Lightweight alternative to ``browse-folders`` for showing quick-pick
chips without the overhead of enumerating a directory tree.  Returns
paths that actually exist on disk (HF cache, LM Studio, Ollama,
``~/models``, etc.) so the frontend can offer them as one-click
"Recommended" shortcuts in the Custom Folders section.

List immediate subdirectories of *path* for the Custom Folders picker.

The frontend uses this to render a modal folder browser without needing
a native OS dialog (Studio is served over HTTP, so the browser can't
reveal absolute paths on the host). The endpoint is read-only and does
not create, move, or delete anything. It simply enumerates visible
subdirectories so the user can click their way to a folder and hand
the resulting string back to POST `/api/models/scan-folders`.

Sandbox: requests are bounded to the allowlist returned by
:func:`_build_browse_allowlist` (HOME, HF cache, Studio dirs,
registered scan folders, well-known model dirs). Paths outside the
allowlist return 403 so users cannot probe ``/etc``, ``/proc``,
``/root`` (when not HOME), or other sensitive system locations
even if the server process can read them. Symlinks are resolved
via ``os.path.realpath`` before the check, so symlink traversal
cannot escape the sandbox either.

Sorting: directories that look like they hold models come first, then
plain directories, then hidden entries (if `show_hidden=true`).

parameters.name: path

schema.description: Directory to list. If omitted, defaults to the current user's home directory. Tilde (`~`) and relative paths are expanded. Must resolve inside the allowlist of browseable roots (HOME, HF cache, Studio dirs, registered scan folders, well-known model dirs).
schema.title: Path

parameters.description: Directory to list. If omitted, defaults to the current user's home directory. Tilde (`~`) and relative paths are expanded. Must resolve inside the allowlist of browseable roots (HOME, HF cache, Studio dirs, registered scan folders, well-known model dirs).

parameters.name: show_hidden

schema.description: Include entries whose name starts with a dot
schema.title: Show Hidden

parameters.description: Include entries whose name starts with a dot

get.description:
List available models (default models and loaded models).

This endpoint returns the default models and any currently loaded models.

Get configuration for a specific model.

This endpoint wraps the backend load_model_defaults function.

Scan for trained LoRA adapters and exported models.

Returns both training outputs (from outputs_dir) and exported models
(from exports_dir) in a single list, distinguished by source field.

parameters.name: outputs_dir

schema.description: Directory to scan for LoRA adapters
schema.title: Outputs Dir

parameters.description: Directory to scan for LoRA adapters

parameters.name: exports_dir

schema.description: Directory to scan for exported models
schema.title: Exports Dir

parameters.description: Directory to scan for exported models

get.description:
Get the base model for a LoRA adapter.

This endpoint wraps the backend get_base_model_from_lora function.

Check if a model is a vision model.

This endpoint wraps the backend is_vision_model function.

Check if a model is an embedding model.

This endpoint wraps the backend is_embedding_model function.

List available GGUF quantization variants for a HuggingFace repo
or a local directory (e.g. LM Studio model folder).

Returns all available quantization variants (Q4_K_M, Q8_0, BF16, etc.)
with file sizes, whether the model supports vision, and the recommended
default variant.

Return download progress by checking cached GGUF files for a specific variant.

Tracks completed shard downloads in snapshots and in-progress downloads
in the blobs directory (incomplete files).

parameters.name: expected_bytes

schema.description: Expected total download size in bytes
schema.title: Expected Bytes

parameters.description: Expected total download size in bytes

get.description:
Return download progress for any HuggingFace model repo.

Checks the local HF cache for completed blobs and in-progress
(.incomplete) downloads. Uses the HF API to determine the expected
total size on the first call, then caches it for subsequent polls.
Also returns ``cache_path``: the realpath of the snapshot directory
(or the cache repo root if no snapshot exists yet) so the UI can
show users where the weights actually live on disk.
List GGUF repos downloaded to HF cache, legacy Unsloth cache, and HF default cache.
List non-GGUF model repos downloaded to HF cache, legacy Unsloth cache, and HF default cache.

delete.description:
Delete a cached model repo (or a specific GGUF variant) from the HF cache.

When *variant* is provided, only the GGUF files matching that quant label
are removed (e.g. ``UD-Q4_K_XL``).  Otherwise the entire repo is deleted.
Refuses if the model is currently loaded for inference.

get.description:
List available checkpoints in the outputs directory.

Scans the outputs folder for training runs and their checkpoints.

post.description:
Load a model for inference.

The model_path should be a clean identifier from GET /models/list.
Returns inference configuration parameters (temperature, top_p, top_k, min_p)
from the model's YAML config, falling back to default.yaml for missing values.

GGUF models are loaded via llama-server (llama.cpp) instead of Unsloth.

Lightweight validation endpoint for model identifiers.

This checks that ModelConfig.from_identifier() can resolve the given
model_path, but it does NOT actually load model weights into GPU memory.

Unload a model from memory.
Routes to the correct backend (llama-server for GGUF, Unsloth otherwise).

Generate a chat response with Server-Sent Events (SSE) streaming.

For vision models, provide image_base64 with the base64-encoded image.

get.description:
Get current inference backend status.
Reports whichever backend (Unsloth or llama-server) is currently active.

Return the active GGUF load's mmap/upload progress.

During the warmup window after a GGUF download -- when llama-server
is paging ~tens-to-hundreds of GB of shards into the page cache
before pushing layers to VRAM -- ``/api/inference/status`` only
shows a generic spinner. This endpoint exposes sampled progress so
the UI can render a real bar plus rate/ETA during that window.

Returns an empty payload (``phase=null, bytes=0``) when no load is
in flight. The frontend should stop polling once ``phase`` becomes
``ready``.

post.description:
Generate audio (TTS) from the latest user message.
Returns a JSON response with base64-encoded WAV audio.
Works with both GGUF (llama-server) and Unsloth/transformers backends.

OpenAI-compatible chat completions endpoint.

Supports multimodal messages: ``content`` may be a plain string or a
list of content parts (``text`` / ``image_url``).

Streaming (default):  returns SSE chunks matching OpenAI's format.
Non-streaming:        returns a single ChatCompletion JSON object.

Automatically routes to the correct backend:
- GGUF models → llama-server via LlamaCppBackend
- Other models → Unsloth/transformers via InferenceBackend

get.description: Serve image files created by Python tool execution. Accepts auth via Authorization header OR ?token= query param (needed because cannot send custom headers).

OpenAI-compatible model listing endpoint.

Returns the currently loaded model in the format expected by
OpenAI-compatible clients (``GET /v1/models``).

post.description:
OpenAI-compatible text completions endpoint (non-chat).

Transparently proxies to the running llama-server's ``/v1/completions``.
Only available when a GGUF model is loaded.

OpenAI-compatible embeddings endpoint.

Transparently proxies to the running llama-server's ``/v1/embeddings``.
Only available when a GGUF model is loaded.
Note: the loaded model must support pooling; otherwise llama-server
will return an error (expected).

OpenAI Responses API endpoint.

Accepts the Responses-format request, converts it to a
ChatCompletionRequest internally, and returns a response
matching the OpenAI Responses API schema (output array,
input_tokens/output_tokens, named SSE events for streaming).

Anthropic-compatible Messages API endpoint.

Translates Anthropic message format to internal OpenAI format, runs
through the existing agentic tool loop when tools are provided, and
returns responses in Anthropic Messages API format (streaming SSE or
non-streaming JSON).

Load a model for inference.

The model_path should be a clean identifier from GET /models/list.
Returns inference configuration parameters (temperature, top_p, top_k, min_p)
from the model's YAML config, falling back to default.yaml for missing values.

GGUF models are loaded via llama-server (llama.cpp) instead of Unsloth.

Lightweight validation endpoint for model identifiers.

This checks that ModelConfig.from_identifier() can resolve the given
model_path, but it does NOT actually load model weights into GPU memory.

Unload a model from memory.
Routes to the correct backend (llama-server for GGUF, Unsloth otherwise).

Generate a chat response with Server-Sent Events (SSE) streaming.

For vision models, provide image_base64 with the base64-encoded image.

Get current inference backend status.

Return the active GGUF load's mmap/upload progress.

shows a generic spinner. This endpoint exposes sampled progress so

Returns an empty payload (``phase=null, bytes=0``) when no load is

get.description:
Return download progress for a HuggingFace dataset repo.

Mirrors ``GET /api/models/download-progress`` but scans the
``datasets--owner--name`` cache directory under HF_HUB_CACHE.
Modern ``datasets``/``huggingface_hub`` caches both raw model and
raw dataset blobs in HF_HUB_CACHE; the ``datasets`` library writes
its processed Arrow shards elsewhere, but the in-progress *download*
bytes are observable here. Returns ``cache_path`` so the UI can
show users where the dataset blobs landed on disk.

post.description:
Check if a dataset requires manual column mapping.

Strategy for HuggingFace datasets:
  1. list_repo_files → pick the first data file → load_dataset(data_files=[…])
     Avoids resolving thousands of files; typically ~2-4 s.
  2. Full streaming load_dataset as a last-resort fallback.

Local files are loaded directly.

Using a plain `def` (not async) so FastAPI runs this in a thread-pool,
preventing any blocking IO from freezing the event loop.

Run LLM-assisted dataset conversion advisor (user-triggered).

Multi-pass analysis using a 7B helper model:
  Pass 1: Classify dataset type from HF card + samples
  Pass 2: Generate conversion strategy (system prompt, templates)
  Pass 3: Validate conversion quality

Falls back to simple column classification if the advisor fails.

Load a checkpoint into the export backend.

Wraps ExportBackend.load_checkpoint.

Cleanup export-related models from memory (GPU/CPU).

Wraps ExportBackend.cleanup_memory.

get.description: Get current export backend status (loaded checkpoint, model type, PEFT flag).

post.description:
Export a merged PEFT model (e.g., 16-bit or 4-bit) and optionally push to Hub.

Wraps ExportBackend.export_merged_model.

Export a non-PEFT base model and optionally push to Hub.

Wraps ExportBackend.export_base_model.

Export the current model to GGUF format and optionally push to Hub.

Wraps ExportBackend.export_gguf.

Export only the LoRA adapter (if the loaded model is PEFT).

Wraps ExportBackend.export_lora_adapter.

get.description:
Stream live stdout/stderr output from the export worker subprocess
as Server-Sent Events.

Events:
  - `log`      : a single log line (data: {"stream","line","ts"})
  - `heartbeat`: periodic keepalive when no new lines are available
  - `complete` : emitted once the export worker is idle and no new
                 lines arrived for ~1 second. Clients should close.
  - `error`    : unrecoverable server-side error

The `id:` field on each event is the log entry's monotonic seq
number so the browser can resume via `Last-Event-ID` on reconnect.

parameters.name: since

schema.description: Return log entries with seq strictly greater than this cursor.
schema.title: Since

parameters.description: Return log entries with seq strictly greater than this cursor.

get.description: List training runs, newest first.
Get a single training run with full config and metrics.

delete.description: Delete a training run and its metrics (CASCADE).

get.description: Health check endpoint

post.description:
Gracefully shut down the Unsloth Studio server.

Called by the frontend quit dialog so users can stop the server from the UI
without needing to use the CLI or kill the process manually.

get.description: Get system information
Return GPU name, total VRAM, and key ML package versions.

initialized.title: Initialized

requires_password_change.title: Requires Password Change
requires_password_change.description: True if the seeded admin must still change the default password

AuthStatusResponse.title: AuthStatusResponse
AuthStatusResponse.description: Indicate whether the seeded admin auth flow is ready.

has_models.title: Has Models
has_models.description: Hint that the directory likely contains models (*.gguf, *.safetensors, config.json, or HF-style `models--*` subfolders). Used by the UI to highlight promising candidates; the scanner itself is authoritative.

BrowseEntry.title: BrowseEntry
BrowseEntry.description: A directory entry surfaced by the folder browser.

current.title: Current
current.description: Absolute path of the directory just listed

parent.title: Parent
parent.description: Parent directory of `current`, or null if `current` is the filesystem root. The frontend uses this to render an `Up` row.

entries.title: Entries
entries.description: Subdirectories of `current`. Sorted with model-bearing directories first, then alphabetically case-insensitive; hidden entries come last within each group.

suggestions.title: Suggestions
suggestions.description: Handy starting points (home, HF cache, already-registered scan folders). Rendered as quick-pick chips above the list.

truncated.title: Truncated
truncated.description: True when the listing was capped because the directory had more subfolders than the server is willing to enumerate in one request. The UI should show a hint telling the user to narrow their path.

model_files_here.title: Model Files Here
model_files_here.description: Count of GGUF/safetensors files immediately inside ``current``. Used by the UI to surface a hint on leaf model directories (which otherwise look `empty` because they contain only files, no subdirectories).

BrowseFoldersResponse.title: BrowseFoldersResponse
BrowseFoldersResponse.description: Response schema for the folder browser endpoint.

current_password.title: Current Password
current_password.description: Existing password for the authenticated user

ChangePasswordRequest.title: ChangePasswordRequest
ChangePasswordRequest.description: Change the current user's password, typically on first login.

model.title: Model
model.description: Model identifier (informational; the active model is used)

stream.title: Stream
stream.description: Whether to stream the response via SSE

max_tokens.title: Max Tokens
max_tokens.description: Maximum tokens to generate (None = until EOS)

use_adapter.title: Use Adapter
use_adapter.description: [x-unsloth] Adapter control for compare mode. null = no change (default), false = disable adapters (base model), true = enable the current adapter, string = enable a specific adapter by name.

enable_thinking.title: Enable Thinking
enable_thinking.description: [x-unsloth] Enable/disable thinking/reasoning mode for supported models

enable_tools.title: Enable Tools
enable_tools.description: [x-unsloth] Enable tool calling for supported models

enabled_tools.title: Enabled Tools
enabled_tools.description: [x-unsloth] List of enabled tool names (e.g. ['web_search', 'python', 'terminal']). If None, all tools are enabled.

auto_heal_tool_calls.title: Auto Heal Tool Calls
auto_heal_tool_calls.description: [x-unsloth] Auto-detect and fix malformed tool calls from model output.

max_tool_calls_per_message.title: Max Tool Calls Per Message
max_tool_calls_per_message.description: [x-unsloth] Maximum number of tool call iterations per message (0 = disabled, 9999 = unlimited).

tool_call_timeout.title: Tool Call Timeout
tool_call_timeout.description: [x-unsloth] Timeout in seconds for each tool call execution (9999 = no limit).

session_id.title: Session Id
session_id.description: [x-unsloth] Session/thread ID for scoping tool execution sandbox.

OpenAI-compatible chat completion request.

Extensions (non-OpenAI fields) are marked with 'x-unsloth'.

content parts for multimodal messages (OpenAI vision format).

Progress of the active GGUF load, sampled on demand.

Used by the UI to show a real progress bar during the
post-download warmup window (mmap + CUDA upload), rather than a
generic "Starting model..." spinner that freezes for minutes on
large MoE models.

max_seq_length.title: Max Seq Length
max_seq_length.description: Maximum sequence length (0 = model default for GGUF)

is_lora.title: Is Lora
is_lora.description: Whether this is a LoRA adapter

trust_remote_code.title: Trust Remote Code
trust_remote_code.description: Allow loading models with custom code (e.g. NVIDIA Nemotron). Only enable for repos you trust.

chat_template_override.title: Chat Template Override
chat_template_override.description: Custom Jinja2 chat template to use instead of the model's default

cache_type_kv.title: Cache Type Kv
cache_type_kv.description: KV cache data type for both K and V (e.g. 'f16', 'bf16', 'q8_0', 'q4_1', 'q5_1')

gpu_ids.title: Gpu Ids
gpu_ids.description: Physical GPU indices to use, for example [0, 1]. Omit or pass [] to use automatic selection. Explicit gpu_ids are unsupported when the parent CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES uses UUID/MIG entries. Not supported for GGUF models.

speculative_type.title: Speculative Type
speculative_type.description: Speculative decoding mode for GGUF models (e.g. 'ngram-simple', 'ngram-mod'). Ignored for non-GGUF and vision models.

LoadRequest.title: LoadRequest
LoadRequest.description: Request to load a model for inference

is_vision.title: Is Vision
is_vision.description: Whether model is a vision model

is_lora.title: Is Lora
is_lora.description: Whether model is a LoRA adapter

is_gguf.title: Is Gguf
is_gguf.description: Whether model is a GGUF model (llama.cpp)

is_audio.title: Is Audio
is_audio.description: Whether model is a TTS audio model

audio_type.title: Audio Type
audio_type.description: Audio codec type: snac, csm, bicodec, dac

requires_trust_remote_code.title: Requires Trust Remote Code
requires_trust_remote_code.description: Whether the model defaults require trust_remote_code to be enabled for loading.

context_length.title: Context Length
context_length.description: Model's native context length (from GGUF metadata)

max_context_length.title: Max Context Length
max_context_length.description: Maximum context length currently available on this hardware

native_context_length.title: Native Context Length
native_context_length.description: Model's native context length from GGUF metadata (not capped by VRAM)

reasoning_always_on.title: Reasoning Always On
reasoning_always_on.description: Whether reasoning is always on (hardcoded <think> tags, not toggleable)

supports_tools.title: Supports Tools
supports_tools.description: Whether model supports tool calling (web search, etc.)

cache_type_kv.title: Cache Type Kv
cache_type_kv.description: KV cache data type for K and V (e.g. 'f16', 'bf16', 'q8_0')

chat_template.title: Chat Template
chat_template.description: Jinja2 chat template string (from GGUF metadata or tokenizer)

speculative_type.title: Speculative Type
speculative_type.description: Active speculative decoding mode (e.g. 'ngram-simple', 'ngram-mod'), or None if disabled

path.title: Path
path.description: Local path where model data was discovered

model_id.title: Model Id
model_id.description: HF repo id for cached models, e.g. org/model

updated_at.title: Updated At
updated_at.description: Unix timestamp of latest observed update

models_dir.title: Models Dir
models_dir.description: Directory scanned for custom local models

hf_cache_dir.title: Hf Cache Dir
hf_cache_dir.description: HF cache root that was scanned

lmstudio_dirs.title: Lmstudio Dirs
lmstudio_dirs.description: LM Studio model directories that were scanned

LocalModelListResponse.title: LocalModelListResponse
LocalModelListResponse.description: Response schema for listing local/cached models.

checkpoints.title: Checkpoints
checkpoints.description: List of checkpoints for this training run (final + intermediate)

base_model.title: Base Model
base_model.description: Base model name from adapter_config.json or config.json

peft_type.title: Peft Type
peft_type.description: PEFT type (e.g. LORA) if adapter training, None for full fine-tune

is_quantized.title: Is Quantized
is_quantized.description: Whether the model uses BNB quantization (e.g. bnb-4bit)

ModelCheckpoints.title: ModelCheckpoints
ModelCheckpoints.description: A training run and its associated checkpoints.

description.title: Description
description.description: Short dataset description for the dataset card

hf_token.title: Hf Token
hf_token.description: Optional Hugging Face token for private or write-protected repos

private.title: Private
private.description: Create or update the dataset repo as private

artifact_path.title: Artifact Path
artifact_path.description: Execution artifact path captured by the UI for completed runs

refresh_token.title: Refresh Token
refresh_token.description: Refresh token from a previous login or refresh

RefreshTokenRequest.title: RefreshTokenRequest
RefreshTokenRequest.description: Refresh token payload to obtain new access + refresh tokens.

ResponsesInputImagePart.title: ResponsesInputImagePart
ResponsesInputImagePart.description: Image content part in a Responses API message (type=input_image).

ResponsesInputMessage.title: ResponsesInputMessage
ResponsesInputMessage.description: A single message in the Responses API input array.

ResponsesInputTextPart.title: ResponsesInputTextPart
ResponsesInputTextPart.description: Text content part in a Responses API message (type=input_text).

stream.title: Stream
stream.description: Whether to stream the response via SSE

ScanFolderInfo.title: ScanFolderInfo
ScanFolderInfo.description: A registered custom model scan folder.

TextContentPart.title: TextContentPart
TextContentPart.description: Text content part in a multimodal message.

access_token.title: Access Token
access_token.description: Session access credential used for authenticated API requests

refresh_token.title: Refresh Token
refresh_token.description: Session refresh credential used to renew an expired access credential

token_type.title: Token Type
token_type.description: Credential type for the Authorization header, always 'bearer'

must_change_password.title: Must Change Password
must_change_password.description: True when the user must change the seeded default password

Token.title: Token
Token.description: Authentication response model for session credentials.

grad_norm_step_history.title: Grad Norm Step History
grad_norm_step_history.description: Step numbers for gradient norm values

TrainingRunDeleteResponse.title: TrainingRunDeleteResponse
TrainingRunDeleteResponse.description: Response for deleting a training run.

TrainingRunDetailResponse.title: TrainingRunDetailResponse
TrainingRunDetailResponse.description: Response for a single training run with config and metrics.

TrainingRunMetrics.title: TrainingRunMetrics
TrainingRunMetrics.description: Metrics arrays for a training run, using paired step arrays per metric.

TrainingRunSummary.title: TrainingRunSummary
TrainingRunSummary.description: Summary of a training run for list views.

trust_remote_code.title: Trust Remote Code
trust_remote_code.description: Allow loading models with custom code (e.g. NVIDIA Nemotron). Only enable for repos you trust.

local_eval_datasets.title: Local Eval Datasets
local_eval_datasets.description: List of local eval dataset paths

eval_steps.title: Eval Steps
eval_steps.description: Fraction of total steps between evals (0-1)

dataset_slice_start.title: Dataset Slice Start
dataset_slice_start.description: Inclusive start row index for dataset slicing

dataset_slice_end.title: Dataset Slice End
dataset_slice_end.description: Inclusive end row index for dataset slicing

custom_format_mapping.title: Custom Format Mapping
custom_format_mapping.description: User-provided column-to-role mapping, e.g. {'image': 'image', 'caption': 'text'} for VLM or {'instruction': 'user', 'output': 'assistant'} for LLM. Enhanced format includes __system_prompt, __user_template, __assistant_template, __label_mapping metadata keys.

is_dataset_image.title: Is Dataset Image
is_dataset_image.description: Whether the dataset contains image data

is_dataset_audio.title: Is Dataset Audio
is_dataset_audio.description: Whether the dataset contains audio data

is_embedding.title: Is Embedding
is_embedding.description: Whether model is an embedding/sentence-transformer model

gpu_ids.title: Gpu Ids
gpu_ids.description: Physical GPU indices to use, for example [0, 1]. Omit or pass [] to use automatic selection. Explicit gpu_ids are unsupported when the parent CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES uses UUID/MIG entries.

TrainingStopResponse.title: TrainingStopResponse
TrainingStopResponse.description: Response for stopping a training job

UploadDatasetResponse.title: UploadDatasetResponse
UploadDatasetResponse.description: Response with stored dataset path for training.

ValidateModelRequest.title: ValidateModelRequest
ValidateModelRequest.description:
Lightweight validation request to check whether a model identifier
*can be resolved* into a ModelConfig.

This does NOT actually load weights into GPU memory.

valid.title: Valid
valid.description: Whether the model identifier looks valid

is_gguf.title: Is Gguf
is_gguf.description: Whether this is a GGUF model (llama.cpp)

is_lora.title: Is Lora
is_lora.description: Whether this is a LoRA adapter

is_vision.title: Is Vision
is_vision.description: Whether this is a vision-capable model

requires_trust_remote_code.title: Requires Trust Remote Code
requires_trust_remote_code.description: Whether the model defaults require trust_remote_code to be enabled for loading.

ValidateModelResponse.title: ValidateModelResponse
ValidateModelResponse.description:
Result of model validation.

valid == True means ModelConfig.from_identifier() succeeded and basic
introspection (GGUF / LoRA / vision flags) is available.

is_vision.title: Is Vision
is_vision.description: Whether the model is a vision model

VisionCheckResponse.title: VisionCheckResponse
VisionCheckResponse.description: Response for checking if a model is a vision model
